subject: [PATCH v2 092/110] ocfs2: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of PRIino format macro being removed and replaced with concrete format strings for u64 values, specifically in mlog messages. The author's response is neutral, providing context that the PRIino macro was already removed and explaining the reasoning behind replacing it with %llu/%llx format strings.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara approved the patch and added a Reviewed-by tag, indicating that he has reviewed it.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the OCFS2 file system to use explicit format strings instead of the PRIino macro, which has been removed since inode numbers are now u64 type. The changes affect various files in the OCFS2 codebase and ensure consistency in formatting inode numbers throughout the system.
